channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: EU Call for Proposals: Information Integrity Research Framework
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
A new funding opportunity has been launched by the European Commission under the European Democracy Shield, aimed at strengthening research on information integrity across the European Union. This call responds to the growing complexity of the digital information environment and the increasing impact of online manipulation on democratic societies. Background A significant portion of public […]

The post EU Call for Proposals: Information Integrity Research Framework appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
A new funding opportunity has been launched by the European Commission under the European Democracy Shield, aimed at strengthening research on information integrity across the European Union.

This call responds to the growing complexity of the digital information environment and the increasing impact of online manipulation on democratic societies.

Background

A significant portion of public discourse now takes place online, where citizens face challenges navigating misinformation, disinformation, and digitally manipulated content.

Recent developments include:

Artificial amplification of content
Misuse of AI to generate and spread deceptive narratives
Cross-platform manipulation strategies
Use of fake websites and misleading digital identities
Exploitation of algorithmic systems to spread divisive content

These trends highlight the urgent need for coordinated research and stronger infrastructure to understand and address threats to information integrity.

About the Call

This call for proposals aims to establish a common research framework that will:

Support advanced research and analysis on information integrity
Enable collaboration across disciplines, sectors, and countries
Strengthen technological infrastructure for research
Improve shared understanding of how manipulation campaigns spread and operate

The initiative is designed as a first step toward long-term structural solutions in safeguarding the digital information ecosystem.

What the Funding Supports

Development of a shared technological backbone for research
Cross-border and interdisciplinary collaboration
Research contributing to joint situational awareness
Expansion of research capabilities in digital information studies

Who Should Apply

This opportunity is suitable for:

Universities and research institutions
Think tanks and policy research organizations
Technology and data-focused organizations
Civil society groups working on media, democracy, or digital governance

Applicants should demonstrate:

Strong expertise in relevant fields such as AI, media studies, or digital policy
Capacity for collaboration across borders and disciplines
Ability to contribute to large-scale research initiatives

Key Dates

Online Information Session: 28 April 2026
Application details available via the EU Funding & Tenders Portal

How to Apply

Access full details and apply here.

For more Grant opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post EU Call for Proposals: Information Integrity Research Framework appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Carnegie China Young Ambassador Internship 2026 (Paid, Singapore) – Apply Now
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Young Ambassador Internship at Carnegie China (Paid Opportunity in Singapore) The Young Ambassador Internship at Carnegie China is now open for applications on a rolling basis, offering students and recent graduates a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in global policy, research, and communications. Based in Singapore, this paid, part-time internship places participants at the […]

The post Carnegie China Young Ambassador Internship 2026 (Paid, Singapore) – Apply Now appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young Ambassador Internship at Carnegie China (Paid Opportunity in Singapore)

The Young Ambassador Internship at Carnegie China is now open for applications on a rolling basis, offering students and recent graduates a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in global policy, research, and communications. Based in Singapore, this paid, part-time internship places participants at the heart of one of Asia-Pacific’s most dynamic intellectual hubs.

About the Internship

The Carnegie China Young Ambassador Internship is designed to provide practical exposure to policy research and strategic communications within a globally respected think tank. Interns will work closely with experts and contribute directly to impactful projects aligned with Carnegie China’s mission.

Internship arrangements are flexible and may vary depending on organizational priorities and the candidate’s background. While primarily based in Singapore, remote participation may be considered for candidates eligible to work in Singapore.

Eligibility Criteria

To be considered, applicants must:

  • Be currently enrolled undergraduate or graduate students, or recent graduates (within two years of graduation)
  • Be eligible to work in Singapore
  • Demonstrate strong academic, analytical, or creative skills depending on the selected track
  • Submit a complete application (resume, cover letter, and writing sample or project pitch)

Note: Positions are highly competitive and limited.

Internship Tracks

Applicants must select one of the following tracks and tailor their application accordingly:

1. Research Track

This track focuses on:

  • Policy analysis and research projects
  • Data-driven initiatives and database development
  • Editorial and publication contributions

Ideal for candidates with:

  • Strong writing and analytical skills
  • Quantitative or research experience

2. Communications (Comms) Track

This track involves:

  • Content creation and digital storytelling
  • Website management and design
  • Event planning and multimedia production

Ideal for candidates with:

  • Technical and digital media skills
  • Creativity and experience with communication tools

Why Apply?

Interning at Carnegie China offers a wide range of professional and personal benefits:

  • Work as a core team member from day one
  • Contribute meaningfully to high-impact global projects
  • Build a strong portfolio through publications and projects
  • Gain exposure to a fast-paced, entrepreneurial work environment
  • Collaborate with a high-performing, globally connected team
  • Access Carnegie’s international network of experts and resources
  • Experience life in Singapore, a leading global hub for policy and innovation

Application Process

To apply, candidates must submit:

  • A resume
  • A cover letter
  • A writing sample (maximum two pages)

Alternatively, applicants may submit a pitch for an original research or communications project in place of or alongside the writing sample.

Click Here, To Apply

Incomplete applications will not be considered.

Shortlisted candidates will undergo a rigorous selection process, which may include assessments and interviews.

Important Details

  • Location: 133 Cecil Street, Singapore
  • Type: Part-time, Paid Internship
  • Application Deadline: 30 September 2026 (EST)
  • Application Mode: Rolling basis

Equal Opportunity

Carnegie China is committed to diversity and inclusion. All qualified applicants will be considered regardless of race, gender, religion, disability, or other protected characteristics.

The Young Ambassador Internship at Carnegie China is an excellent opportunity for aspiring policy professionals, researchers, and communicators looking to gain real-world experience in an international setting. With direct exposure to global issues and access to a prestigious network, this program can be a strong stepping stone toward a career in international affairs.

Discover more similar opportunities here

The post Carnegie China Young Ambassador Internship 2026 (Paid, Singapore) – Apply Now appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Paid Youth Technical Experts Opportunity 2026 ($1,000 Stipend) – African Youth Charter @20 | Apply by April 30
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Looking for a paid youth governance, policy, or research opportunity in Africa? The African Peer Review Mechanism (APRM), in partnership with YouLead Africa and Restless Development, is now accepting applications for 20 Youth Technical Experts to support the African Youth Charter @20 appraisal. Key Details (Read This First) Stipend: $1,000 USD Deadline: 30 April 2026 […]

The post Paid Youth Technical Experts Opportunity 2026 ($1,000 Stipend) – African Youth Charter @20 | Apply by April 30 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Looking for a paid youth governance, policy, or research opportunity in Africa? The African Peer Review Mechanism (APRM), in partnership with YouLead Africa and Restless Development, is now accepting applications for 20 Youth Technical Experts to support the African Youth Charter @20 appraisal.

Key Details (Read This First)

  • Stipend: $1,000 USD
  • Deadline: 30 April 2026 (12:00 SAST)
  • Duration: 3–4 months (May–August 2026)
  • Commitment: ~8 hours/week
  • Eligibility: African youth
  • Location: Remote + country-level engagement
  • Apply via: tender@aprm-au.org

 

TERMS OF REFERENCE – YOUTH TECHNICAL EXPERTS

Call for Applications

APRM/DP/YTE/IC/04/2026: CALL FOR TWENTY (20) YOUTH TECHNICAL EXPERTS TO APPRAISE THE AFRICAN YOUTH CHARTER @20

The Technical proposal must be submitted via email to tender@aprm-au.org on or before 30 April 2026 at 12.00 (SAST). Proposals shall be opened on the 30 of April 2026 at 12.00 (SAST).

A collaborative initiative of the African Peer Review Mechanism Continental Secretariat, YouLead Africa, and Restless Development

1. Programme Background

In 2026, the African Youth Charter (AYC) marks its 20th anniversary, a defining continental milestone that reaffirms Africa’s commitment to the socioeconomic inclusion of youth, democratic participation, and sustainable development. This anniversary presents a critical opportunity to reflect on progress achieved, assess implementation gaps, and redouble efforts to ensure meaningful youth empowerment across the continent.

As Africa navigates profound transformation in governance, peace, development, and justice, young people, who constitute more than 75% of the continent’s population, are the leaders of tomorrow and also active actors shaping Africa today. Against this backdrop, the African Peer Review Mechanism (APRM) Continental Secretariat, YouLead Africa and Restless Development have launched the initiative: “Technical Experts on Youth Governance and Development: Consultations and Policy Briefs on the African Youth Charter @20”.

As part of this initiative, young Africans are invited to serve as Youth Technical Experts to assess and document how African Union Member States, Regional Economic Communities (RECs), National Youth Councils, Civil Society Organisations, Development Partners, Private Sector and governance frameworks are implementing the AYC and related continental and regional commitments. The appraisal’s findings will directly inform a consolidated continental policy brief to be launched during Africa Youth Day commemorations and will contribute to the APRM Africa Governance Report (AGR) 2027, focusing on Youth in Governance: From Promise to Prosperity.

2. Aim of the Youth Technical Experts

Youth Technical Experts will lead participatory, evidence-based assessments of how African Union frameworks, including the AYC, Agenda 2063, the African Charter on Democracy, Elections and Governance (ACDEG), and the Africa Governance Architecture (AGA) Youth Engagement Strategy, are being implemented across the continent. Through a structured appraisal process, Youth Technical Experts will assess institutional responsiveness to youth needs and generate actionable, youth-informed recommendations and best practices to strengthen discourse on continental and regional governance.

3. Objectives of the Youth Technical Experts’ assessments

  • Synthesise youth consultation outcomes from the APRM, YouLead Africa, and the Restless Development Youth Platform processes, capturing emerging perspectives on the implementation of the AYC and related AU frameworks.
  • Analyse governance and participation trends, assessing how AU frameworks, including Agenda 2063, ACDEG, and the AGA Youth Engagement Strategy, have advanced youth inclusion.
  • Identify implementation gaps and document barriers to youth participation at national, regional, and continental levels.
  • Contribute to the consolidated continental policy brief with forward-looking recommendations to strengthen the implementation of the AYC and related AU frameworks.
  • Amplify youth perspectives in continental policy dialogue and contribute to the drafting of the AGR 2027.
  • Alongside the APRM Youth Unit, YouLead Africa, and Restless Development, coordinate consultation processes and ensure outputs align with the AYC @20 commemorations.

4. Thematic Areas of Focus

Youth Technical Experts will assess implementation across the following thematic areas:

  • Youth, Governance and Democratic Transformation: Youth political representation, elections, constitutional reforms, and digital civic engagement.
  • Peace, Security and Youth Agency: Youth inclusion within the AGA – African Peace and Security Architecture (APSA), peacebuilding, and resilience in conflict contexts.
  • Economic Justice, Development and Employment: Youth unemployment, African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) and entrepreneurship, innovation, digital finance, and gig economy.
  • Pan-Africanism, Identity and Youth Mobilisation: Diaspora engagement with the AU, cultural activism, and Afrocentric education.
  • Policy and Institutional Responsiveness: Evaluation of the AU 1 Million Next Level Initiative, youth-led monitoring of Agenda 2063 and the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), and the role of AU organs, RECs, and Member States.

5. Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Be between 18 and 35 years of age at the time of application.
  • Be a national of an African Union Member State.
  • Be active in civil society, youth networks, youth-led organisations, academic institutions, or community-based organisations.
  • Demonstrate a strong interest in youth governance, policy, civic engagement, accountability, or development.
  • Be available to participate in the appraisal process for approximately 3-4 months (May- August 2026).
  • Be proficient in written and spoken English and/or French. Fluency in Portuguese, Arabic, or Swahili is an added advantage.
  • Have prior experience in research, advocacy, community engagement, or policy analysis (preferred but not required; training will be provided).

6. Requirements

The APRM Continental Secretariat, YouLead Africa, and Restless Development seek individuals with demonstrated passion for fostering a youth-led, responsive Africa. Ideal candidates must:

  • Demonstrate an understanding of the lives and experiences of diverse, intersectional, and marginalised youth across Africa.
  • Be passionate about advocacy, governance, meaningful youth engagement, and collaborative movement building.
  • Value inclusivity and be committed to intergenerational partnerships and the representation of underrepresented youth.
  • Demonstrate alignment with the principles of the AYC, Agenda 2063, and AU governance frameworks.

This is an inclusive initiative. We strongly encourage often-excluded groups, including young women, youth with disabilities, and youth from rural and conflict-affected communities, to apply.

7. Incentives

Selected Youth Technical Experts will be afforded:

  • Official recognition as a Youth Technical Expert under the AYC @20 Appraisal, co-convened by the APRM Continental Secretariat, YouLead Africa and Restless Development.
  • A personalised certificate of participation.
  • The opportunity to attend an orientation and capacity-building workshop on the AYC, AU governance frameworks, and appraisal methodologies.
  • Mentorship, well-being support, and a conducive professional development environment throughout the appraisal period.
  • The opportunity to have appraisal findings published in the Consolidated Continental Policy Brief, launched during the International Youth Day commemorations.
  • The opportunity to contribute to the Edited Policy Volume on Youth Governance in Africa (late 2026) and to inform the development of the AGR 2027.
  • Continental networking with youth leaders, governance experts, policymakers, researchers, and civil society partners across Africa.

8. Time Commitment

Youth Technical Experts are expected to dedicate approximately 3–4 months (May–August 2026), 8 hours per week. This includes:

  • Participation in the orientation and appraisal tool development sessions (late May 2026).
  • Participation in structured virtual consultations convened through the APRM, YouLead Africa and Restless Development Youth Platforms (May–June 2026).
  • Fieldwork and data collection in their national or regional context.
  • Data analysis and drafting of structured appraisal reports (due on 17 July 2026).
  • Participation in the International Youth Day Policy Launch Event.

9. Remuneration, payment schedule and deliverables

  • 50% of the Draft report based on online consultation is due within 30 days of the start of the project.
  • Successful candidates will receive a total stipend of USD 1000 for the duration of the assignment in addition to funding to cover assignment costs.

10. Project timelines

  1. Application deadline: April 30, 2026
  2. Notification of selected Youth Technical Experts: May 7, 2026
  3. Virtual Orientation and Training Workshop: May 11, 2026
  4. Continental Consultation and Appraisal Period: May–30 June 2026
  5. Submission of Appraisal Reports: July 17, 2026
  6. International Youth Day Policy Launch: August 12, 2026

11. Selection Process

Shortlisted candidates will be invited to interviews and additional assessments. Selection will follow a transparent and fair process anchored on diversity and inclusion, with attention paid to geographic representation across AU Member States, gender equity, and the inclusion of underrepresented youth. All applicants will be afforded the same treatment.

12. How to Apply

Interested candidates must submit:

  • a letter of motivation and interest (maximum 500 words), outlining: (a) why they want to serve as a Youth Technical Expert; (b) their knowledge of or engagement with youth governance and the African Youth Charter; and (c) the thematic area(s) and country/region they will focus on;
  • A short biography (maximum 150 words) stating their name, age, nationality, affiliation (if any), and contact details;
  • A curriculum vitae; and
  • Scanned copies of qualifications and relevant academic credentials to be submitted electronically via email to: tender@aprm-au.org.

The Technical proposal must be submitted via email to tender@aprm-au.org on or before 30 April 2026 at 12:00 (SAST). Proposals shall be opened on 30 April 2026 at 12.00 (SAST).

SUBMISSIONS MUST BE TITLED:APRM/DP/YTE/IC/04/2026: CALL FOR YOUTH TECHNICAL EXPERTS TO APPRAISE THE AFRICAN YOUTH CHARTER @20

All applications must be in writing and accompanied by the relevant documents as prescribed above.

African Peer Review Mechanism

Physical Address: 349 Witch-Hazel, Building F, Eco Origin office park.

Tel: +27 11 256 3400/01/29

Fax: +2711 256 3456

Submission Email: tender@aprm-au.org

Download PDF: TERMS OF REFERENCE – YOUTH TECHNICAL EXPERTS

Terms of Reference (TOR)

If you’re passionate about youth leadership, governance, and policy, this is your chance to contribute at a continental level.Apply before 30 April 2026

 Explore More Opportunities for Africans 👉 https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/?s=Africa

The post Paid Youth Technical Experts Opportunity 2026 ($1,000 Stipend) – African Youth Charter @20 | Apply by April 30 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Trade Organization (WTO) Young Professionals Programme 2027 (Paid – CHF 4,000/Month) | Apply Now (Open to Several Nationalities)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Key Details (Quick Overview) Organization: World Trade Organization (WTO) Location: Geneva, Switzerland Programme Type: Young Professionals Programme (Full-time) Salary: CHF 4,000 per month Duration: 1 year (non-renewable) Application Deadline: 7 May 2026 Eligibility: Advanced degree + minimum 2 years experience Who Can Apply: Nationals of WTO member countries (priority to developing/LDC countries) Language: English required […]

The post World Trade Organization (WTO) Young Professionals Programme 2027 (Paid – CHF 4,000/Month) | Apply Now (Open to Several Nationalities) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Key Details (Quick Overview)

  • Organization: World Trade Organization (WTO)
  • Location: Geneva, Switzerland
  • Programme Type: Young Professionals Programme (Full-time)
  • Salary: CHF 4,000 per month
  • Duration: 1 year (non-renewable)
  • Application Deadline: 7 May 2026
  • Eligibility: Advanced degree + minimum 2 years experience
  • Who Can Apply: Nationals of WTO member countries (priority to developing/LDC countries)
  • Language: English required (French/Spanish is a plus)

The WTO Young Professionals Programme (YPP) 2027 is a prestigious, paid international opportunity designed for talented young professionals (aged 32 or under by 1 January 2027) seeking hands-on experience in global trade and policy.

Participants will work directly within the WTO Secretariat in Geneva, contributing to real-world trade issues while building expertise in the multilateral trading system.

The WTO seeks to attract and retain staff members offering the highest standards of competence, efficiency and integrity and to build an organization that is representative of the Members and People it serves. As an equal opportunity and inclusive employer, the WTO gives full regard to competence and welcome applications from qualified candidates irrespective of age, disability status, gender, nationality, race, religion, sexual orientation, social origin or any other characteristic. Applications from non-represented Members (link) are particularly encouraged for all positions.

The Secretariat of the WTO is seeking candidates for THE WORLD TRADE ORGANIZATION YOUNG PROFESSIONALS PROGRAMME for 2027.

 

The WTO Young Professionals Programme (YPP) is a unique opportunity for qualified young professionals up to the age of 32 years, as at 1 January 2027, from WTO Members with low or no professional representation in the WTO Secretariat, to enhance their knowledge of WTO and skills in international trade issues.  The Programme aims to widen the pool of professionals from these WTO Members, with the ultimate goal of broadening their representation in the WTO and/or other regional and international organizations. Further information on the YPP can be found on the WTO website at https://www.wto.org/english/thewto_e/vacan_e/ypp_e.htm.

 

ALL APPLICATIONS MUST BE SUBMITTED IN ENGLISH, ACCOMPANIED BY A COVER LETTER OF MOTIVATION, WHICH SHOULD INDICATE UP TO THREE AREAS OF WORK (SEE DETAILS UNDER GENERAL FUNCTIONS), IN ORDER OF PREFERENCE, THAT THE APPLICANT WOULD BE INTERESTED IN. Applications with no accompanying letter of motivation will not be considered.

 

This programme offers selected young professionals the opportunity to gain work experience in the WTO. It is a one-year programme without possibility of extension.

 

 

General Functions

 

The selected Young Professional will be placed in a specific Division of the WTO Secretariat in accordance with the needs and priorities of the Organization; and based on the identified areas of interest of the Young Professional. Each Young Professional may express interest in no more than three areas of WTO work; and should state their order of preference.

 

The areas of work may include, inter alia, the following (in alphabetical order):

 

– Accessions

– Agriculture

– Communications and External Relations

– Competition Policy

– Council and Trade Negotiations

– Digital Trade

– Dispute Settlement

– Economic Research and Statistics

– Fisheries Subsidies

– Government Procurement (GPA)

– Intellectual Property Rights

– Investment Facilitation for Development

– Micro, Small and Medium-sized Enterprises (MSMEs)

– Market Access (tariffs and non-tariff barriers)

– Sanitary and Phytosanitary measures

– Technical Barriers to Trade

– Trade and Development

– Trade and Environment/Climate Change

– Trade Facilitation

– Trade Finance

– Trade and Gender

– Trade in Services and Investment

– Trade Policy Analysis

– Trade-Related Technical Assistance.

– Trade Remedies (anti-dumping and countervailing duties)

 

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 

Education:

 

Advanced university degree in economics, econometrics, international relations, law, environment studies, or other trade-related subjects relevant to the work of the WTO (e.g. international investment law, trade and gender, etc.). Applicants must have obtained their advanced university degree by the time they submit their application.

 

Knowledge and Skills:

 

General knowledge of the work of the WTO.

Technical knowledge in one or more of the areas of work indicated above.

Good drafting skills in English. Ability to draft in French and/or Spanish would be an advantage.

Ability to work independently and as part of a team, in a multicultural environment.

Demonstrated strong interest in international trade and the multilateral trading system.

Commitment and passion for trade or WTO-related work.

 

Work Experience:

 

Minimum two (2) years of professional experience relevant to the work of the WTO.

 

Languages:

 

Fluency in English. A good working knowledge of either French or Spanish would be an advantage.

 

Information:

 

Preference shall be given to nationals from WTO Members without any representation as well as from developing and least developed countries (LDCs) Members with low representation (up to three staff members) at the professional level in the Secretariat (list below). Priority will be given to WTO Members from developing and LDCs that have no representation and have not participated in previous cohorts of the YPP (marked with an asterisk in list below).

 

Albania, Angola, Armenia, Kingdom of Bahrain, Bangladesh, Barbados, Belize, Benin*, Plurinational State of Bolivia*, Botswana, Brunei Darussalam*, Burkina Faso, Burundi, Cabo Verde*, Cambodia, Chile, Comoros*, Costa Rica, Côte d’Ivoire, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Democratic Republic of the Congo*, Djibouti*, Dominican Republic, El Salvador, Eswatini, Fiji, Gabon*, The Gambia, Georgia, Grenada, Guatemala, Guyana, Haiti, Honduras, Hong Kong China, Iceland, Indonesia, Israel, Jamaica, Jordan, Kazakhstan, the State of Kuwait*, Kyrgyz Republic*, Lao People’s Democratic Republic*, Latvia, Lesotho, Liberia, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Macao China, Madagascar, Malawi, Malaysia, Maldives, Mali, Malta, Mauritania, Mauritius, Republic of Moldova, Mongolia, Montenegro, Morocco, Mozambique, Myanmar*, Namibia, Nepal, Nicaragua, Niger*, Nigeria, North Macedonia, Norway, Oman, Pakistan, Panama, Papua New Guinea, Paraguay, Qatar*, Rwanda, Saint Kitts and Nevis*, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Samoa*, Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, Senegal, Seychelles, Sierra Leone*, Singapore, Slovak Republic, Slovenia, Solomon Islands*, South Africa, Sri Lanka, Suriname*, Chinese Taipei, Tajikistan, Tanzania, Thailand, Timor-Leste*, Togo, Tonga*, Uganda, Ukraine, United Arab Emirates*, Uruguay, Vanuatu*, Viet Nam, Yemen*, Zambia, Zimbabwe.

FIND OUT MORE AND APPLY NOW 

Only applications from nationals of WTO Members will be accepted. Female candidates are strongly encouraged to apply to this Programme.

 

The WTO may use various communication technologies such as video or teleconference for the assessment and evaluation of candidates. The recruitment process may also involve the use of various forms of testing, assessment centres, interviews and reference checks.

Additional Information:
Only applications from nationals of WTO Members will be accepted.

The WTO may use various communication technologies such as video or teleconference for the assessment and evaluation of candidates. The recruitment process may also involve the use of various forms of testing, assessment centres, interviews and reference checks.

All applicants are encouraged to apply online as soon as possible after the vacancy has been posted and well before the closing date – Geneva (Switzerland) time – stated in the vacancy announcement.

FIND OUT MORE AND APPLY NOW 

PLEASE NOTE THAT APPLICATIONS RECEIVED AFTER THE CLOSING DATE WILL NOT BE ACCEPTED.

The WTO is a non-smoking environment.

 

You may also be interested in these WTO opportunities:

  • Call for Applications: United Nations WTO Young Trade Leaders 2026 | Gain Global Exposure, Training in Geneva and a Platform to Lead on Trade! https://wp.me/p23f03-ag0
  • WTO Internship Opportunities for Post-Graduate Students https://wp.me/p23f03-iTO

The post World Trade Organization (WTO) Young Professionals Programme 2027 (Paid – CHF 4,000/Month) | Apply Now (Open to Several Nationalities) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: CBCF Congressional Fellowship and Internship Program 2026: Paid Policy Internships and Fellowship in Washington, D.C. ($60,000–$70,000 Salary)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Key Highlights Host Organization: Congressional Black Caucus Foundation (CBCF) Program Type: Paid Fellowship (Public Policy & Legislative Leadership) Duration: 12 months Location: Washington, D.C. (relocation required) Salary: $60,000–$70,000 per year + benefits Fellowship Types: Congressional, John R. Lewis Social Justice, Research Benefits: Interim housing (July–August), professional development, mentorship, alumni network (8,000+) Eligibility: Early-career professionals interested […]

The post CBCF Congressional Fellowship and Internship Program 2026: Paid Policy Internships and Fellowship in Washington, D.C. ($60,000–$70,000 Salary) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Key Highlights

  • Host Organization: Congressional Black Caucus Foundation (CBCF)
  • Program Type: Paid Fellowship (Public Policy & Legislative Leadership)
  • Duration: 12 months
  • Location: Washington, D.C. (relocation required)
  • Salary: $60,000–$70,000 per year + benefits
  • Fellowship Types: Congressional, John R. Lewis Social Justice, Research
  • Benefits: Interim housing (July–August), professional development, mentorship, alumni network (8,000+)
  • Eligibility: Early-career professionals interested in public policy and public service
  • Deadline: 1 May 2026 (for major fellowship tracks)
  • Additional Opportunities: Paid internships also available (up to $4,000)

About the CBCF Congressional Fellowship Program

The CBCF Congressional Fellowship Program is a highly competitive leadership development initiative designed to prepare early-career professionals for impactful roles in public policy and governance. Over a 12-month period, fellows gain direct, hands-on experience working within the U.S. legislative system.

Participants serve as full-time legislative aides and policy analysts, contributing to real policy work that shapes national conversations. The program combines practical experience with structured training, ensuring fellows develop both technical and leadership skills.

The Congressional Black Caucus Foundation is a non-partisan 501(c)(3) organization that provides scholarships, internships, and fellowships to support the next generation of leaders in public service.

Fellowship Types

Congressional Fellowships

Fellows spend six months working in the offices of members of the Congressional Black Caucus, followed by a six-month placement with a congressional committee or subcommittee aligned with their interests.

John R. Lewis Social Justice Fellowships

Participants complete a six-month placement in a CBC member office and then transition to the CBCF Center for Policy Analysis and Research, where they conduct research on key social justice issues.

Research Fellowships

Fellows focus entirely on research for the full 12 months, contributing to policy analysis and publications through CBCF’s research center.

Salary and Benefits

The fellowship offers a competitive compensation package and valuable career development opportunities:

  • Annual salary ranging from $60,000 to $70,000
  • Benefits package
  • Interim housing support during the first two months (July and August)
  • Monthly professional development sessions
  • Access to corporate sponsors and mentorship
  • Opportunities to expand professional networks
  • Membership in an alumni network of more than 8,000 professionals

What Fellows Do

Fellows play an active role in policy development and legislative processes. Responsibilities typically include:

  • Drafting memos, speeches, and op-eds
  • Conducting research for proposed legislation
  • Supporting congressional offices and committees
  • Engaging with constituents and stakeholders
  • Contributing to legislative portfolios and policy discussions

This immersive experience equips fellows with the skills needed for careers in public policy, government, and advocacy.

CBCF Internships Also Available

In addition to fellowships, the Congressional Black Caucus Foundation offers several paid internship programs for students and early-career professionals. These internships provide valuable entry points into policy, communications, and leadership pathways.

Current Internship Opportunities

  • CBCF State Farm Communications Internship Program
    • Stipend: $4,000
    • Duration: Semester-based
    • Deadline: 26 June 2026
  • CBCF Walmart Emerging Leaders Internship Program
    • Stipend: $4,000
    • Duration: Semester-based
    • Deadline: 26 June 2026
  • CBCF Pathways to the C-Suite Internship Program
    • Stipend: $4,000
    • Duration: Semester-based
    • Deadline: 26 June 2026

These internships are ideal for individuals looking to gain foundational experience in leadership, communications, and professional development within a policy-focused environment.

Eligibility and Requirements

The program is designed for early-career professionals with a strong interest in public policy and public service. Applicants should demonstrate strong research, analytical, and communication skills.

Participants must be willing to relocate to Washington, D.C., and are responsible for their own travel and living expenses beyond the initial housing support.

Immediate relatives of Congressional Black Caucus members, staff, CBCF staff, board members, or affiliated sponsors are not eligible to apply.

Career Impact and Alumni Outcomes

The CBCF Fellowship has a strong reputation for launching careers in public service and policy. Alumni have gone on to hold influential roles across government, nonprofit organizations, and the private sector.

The program provides not only practical experience but also long-term professional connections that support career advancement.

Why You Should Apply

The CBCF Congressional Fellowship Program is one of the most respected paid policy fellowships in the United States. It offers a unique combination of hands-on legislative experience, professional training, and networking opportunities.

For aspiring policy professionals, it provides a direct pathway into government and public service careers.

How to Apply

Applicants should visit the official Congressional Black Caucus Foundation website to review detailed application requirements, deadlines, and submission guidelines. Candidates are required to create an account to apply for available opportunities.

APPLY NOW 

Final Thoughts

The CBCF Congressional Fellowship Program 2026 stands out as a leading opportunity for those seeking to build a career in public policy and governance. With competitive funding, real-world legislative experience, and access to a powerful professional network, it remains a top choice for early-career professionals globally. Find out more here https://www.cbcfinc.org/programs/fellowships/

The post CBCF Congressional Fellowship and Internship Program 2026: Paid Policy Internships and Fellowship in Washington, D.C. ($60,000–$70,000 Salary) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: House of Lords Social Mobility Work Experience Programme 2026 (UK) – Apply Now
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Are you a Year 12 student in the UK looking to gain real-world experience inside Parliament? Applications are now open for the House of Lords Social Mobility Work Experience Programme 2026 — a unique opportunity to explore careers in public service, policy, and governance. Delivered by the House of Lords, this one-week immersive programme gives […]

The post House of Lords Social Mobility Work Experience Programme 2026 (UK) – Apply Now appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a Year 12 student in the UK looking to gain real-world experience inside Parliament? Applications are now open for the House of Lords Social Mobility Work Experience Programme 2026 — a unique opportunity to explore careers in public service, policy, and governance.

Delivered by the House of Lords, this one-week immersive programme gives students a behind-the-scenes look at how Parliament operates and how laws are shaped in the UK.

Key Details at a Glance

    • Host: House of Lords
    • Programme Type: Work Experience (Social Mobility Programme)
    • Location: London, United Kingdom
    • Duration: 1 Week
    • Eligibility: Year 12 students (UK)
    • Application Deadline: 22 April 2026

 

Programme Overview

The House of Lords Social Mobility Work Experience Programme offers selected students the chance to:

  • Experience Parliament beyond what you see in the news
  • Learn how the House of Lords functions in practice
  • Engage directly with professionals across departments
  • Participate in real workplace activities and projects

This is an ideal opportunity for students interested in politics, governance, communications, or public service careers.

The House of Lords is running a social mobility work experience programme for Year 12 students. 

The House of Lords Social Mobility Work Experience Programme will offer students the opportunity to go beyond the headlines and experience Parliament from the inside. Over one exciting week, participants will explore how the House of Lords operates.

The programme includes:

  • Working alongside professionals in key departments
  • Taking part in hands-on tasks and real workplace activities
  • Shadowing staff and seeing how different roles come together behind the scenes
  • Joining interactive workshops designed to build your skills and confidence.

What you’ll gain:

  • A clear understanding of careers in Parliament
  • Practical workplace experience you can talk about in applications
  • Confidence, connections and inspiration.

Whether you’re curious about politics, interested in public service or just want to try something new, this programme could be for you.

Offices involved

Property and Office Services

This team is responsible for front of house services, houskeeping, heritage cleaning and accommodation as well as managing contracts for the parliamentary estate and supporting work on ceremonial events and maintenance projects.

Black Rod’s department

The team plays a leading role in all ceremonial events such as the State Opening of Parliament and state visits, as well as supporting the day to day running of the House of Lords and controlling access to the chamber.

Clerk of the Parliament’s Office

The office supports the Clerk of the Parliaments to carry out their duties as head of the Administration, plus other key officeholders such as the Senior Deputy Speaker, Convenor of the Crossbench Peers, Clerk Assistant and Chief Operating Officer. The office also includes teams responsible for governance, internal audit, interparliamentary relations, and Restoration and Renewal of the Palace of Westminster.

Committees

The Commitee Office provides support for Lords select committees; small groups of members investigating areas of public policy. The team provides committees with the policy, procedural, logistical, administrative and legal support they need to carry out their work.

Communications

Communications is responsible for explaining the role and work of the House of Lords to the public,  increasing awareness and improving understanding of the the value it adds. The team includes press and media, digital communications, engagement, public enquiries and internal communications.

How to apply

For full details and to submit your application, visit the Futures for All website https://finder.futuresforall.org/opportunity-detail/6F87378B-EBBA-4F4A-04AD-08DE8A5A1670

The deadline to apply is Wednesday 22 April 2026.

About the House of Lords

The House of Lords is the upper chamber of the UK Parliament. It is independent from, and complements the work of, the elected House of Commons. The Lords shares the task of making and shaping laws and checking and challenging the work of the government.

Application Link: https://finder.futuresforall.org/opportunity-detail/6F87378B-EBBA-4F4A-04AD-08DE8A5A1670

For more opportunities for young people in the UK and Europe please visit: https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/?s=Europe

The post House of Lords Social Mobility Work Experience Programme 2026 (UK) – Apply Now appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA) Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026– Open for Applications
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The 2026 Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA) Postdoctoral Fellowships are now open, offering €399.05 million in funding to support researchers worldwide. These prestigious fellowships provide advanced training, international mobility, and career development opportunities for PhD holders across all disciplines. Key Details at a Glance Programme: MSCA Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 Funding: €399.05 million Deadline: 9 September 2026 […]

The post Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA) Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026– Open for Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The 2026 Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA) Postdoctoral Fellowships are now open, offering €399.05 million in funding to support researchers worldwide.

These prestigious fellowships provide advanced training, international mobility, and career development opportunities for PhD holders across all disciplines.

Key Details at a Glance

  • Programme: MSCA Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026
  • Funding: €399.05 million
  • Deadline: 9 September 2026
  • Launch Date: 9 April 2026
  • Projects Funded: ~1,600 expected
  • Duration:
    • European Fellowships: Up to 24 months
    • Global Fellowships: 12–24 months + 12-month return phase in Europe
  • Fields: All disciplines (including Euratom research)
  • Eligibility: PhD holders with up to 8 years of research experience

About the MSCA Postdoctoral Fellowships

The MSCA Postdoctoral Fellowships are among Europe’s most competitive research funding programmes. They support researchers in gaining new skills, expanding networks, and advancing careers through international, interdisciplinary, and inter-sectoral mobility.

Fellows work with leading institutions worldwide and benefit from strong research collaboration and mentorship.

Types of Fellowships

1. European Postdoctoral Fellowships

  • Open to researchers of any nationality
  • Hosted in EU Member States or Horizon Europe associated countries
  • Duration: Up to 24 months

2. Global Postdoctoral Fellowships

  • Open to EU nationals or long-term residents
  • Research conducted in a third country (12–24 months)
  • Includes a mandatory 12-month return phase in Europe

Additional Opportunity: Non-Academic Placement

Fellows can request an additional 6-month placement in a non-academic organisation (e.g., industry, NGOs, public sector) in Europe, helping bridge research and real-world application.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Hold a PhD at the time of application
  • Have a maximum of 8 years of research experience post-PhD
  • Apply jointly with a host organisation and supervisor

Eligible host organisations include:

  • Universities
  • Research institutes
  • Companies and SMEs
  • Government or public institutions
  • NGOs, hospitals, museums, and other organisations

For Global Fellowships, applicants must also secure a host organisation in a third country.

Who Should Apply?

The programme is open to:

  • Researchers from any country
  • Those seeking to restart their careers
  • Researchers displaced by conflict
  • Researchers aiming to reintegrate into Europe

Conditions for researchers and organisations

MSCA Postdoctoral Fellowships are open to postdoctoral researchers from all over the world, of any nationality and at any career stage, with a maximum of 8 years of research experience after their PhD.

Some exceptions and specific conditions apply, for instance for Global Postdoctoral Fellowships and Euratom research.

Researchers must develop an application with their prospective supervisor and apply together with their future host organisation, which can be

  • a university
  • a research institution or facility
  • a company, small or medium-sized enterprise
  • a government, public institution, or body
  • a museum, hospital, or NGO
  • any other organisation

based in an EU Member State or Horizon Europe associated country.

Researchers applying to Global Fellowships will need to seek the commitment of an organisation based in a third country, as they will carry out their research there for a period of between 12 and 24 months.

The call is open to researchers wishing to reintegrate in Europe, to those displaced by conflict, as well as to researchers with high potential who are seeking to restart their careers.

ERA Fellowships (Additional Funding Opportunity)

Applicants to European Fellowships hosted in “widening countries” can opt into ERA Fellowships.

  • Around 40 additional fellowships available
  • Targeted at excellent applicants not funded due to budget limits
  •  Find the eligible host countries for ERA Fellowships.

Indicative Timeline

  • 9 April 2026: Call opens
  • 9 September 2026: Application deadline
  • February 2027: Results announced (TBC)
  • Feb–May 2027: Grant agreements signed
  • May 2027: Projects begin

Why Apply for MSCA Fellowships?

  • Access world-class research environments
  • Gain international experience
  • Strengthen your academic and non-academic career prospects
  • Build global networks and collaborations
  • Enhance your research impact and visibility

30 Years of MSCA Excellence

2026 marks the 30th anniversary of the MSCA programme.

Since 1996, MSCA has:

  • Supported 150,000+ researchers
  • Contributed to 23 Nobel Prize winners
  • Strengthened global research collaboration and mobility

How to Apply

Applicants must develop a proposal jointly with their host institution and supervisor.

Start early and ensure your proposal demonstrates:

  • Strong research excellence
  • Clear impact
  • Quality training and supervision

Find out more and apply

The MSCA Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 represent one of the best-funded and most prestigious opportunities for early-career researchers globally. With nearly 1,600 projects expected to be funded, this is a major opportunity to advance your research career on an international stage.

Postdoctoral Fellowships call: 6 steps to prepare your application

Visit the 2026 Postdoctoral Fellowship call page

Find out more on how to apply to MSCA

 

The post Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA) Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026– Open for Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded PhD on Youth & AI at KU Leuven (IAmAI Project) – Apply by May 31, 2026
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Artificial Intelligence is rapidly transforming education, identity development, and how young people interact with the world. Yet, many AI systems are still designed without actively involving the very individuals they impact the most—youth. A groundbreaking fully funded PhD opportunity at KU Leuven aims to change that narrative by placing young people at the center of […]

The post Fully Funded PhD on Youth & AI at KU Leuven (IAmAI Project) – Apply by May 31, 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Artificial Intelligence is rapidly transforming education, identity development, and how young people interact with the world. Yet, many AI systems are still designed without actively involving the very individuals they impact the most—youth.

A groundbreaking fully funded PhD opportunity at KU Leuven aims to change that narrative by placing young people at the center of AI development through the innovative IAmAI Project: Youth in the Loop of AI Developments.

About the PhD Opportunity

This four-year doctoral position is hosted at the Centre for Methodology of Educational Sciences (CMPO), within the Faculty of Psychology and Educational Sciences. The research is led by Melissa De Smet, an expert in qualitative and mixed-methods research.

The project focuses on developing participatory methodologies that bridge the gap between how AI systems are built and how young people actually learn, grow, and experience the world.

Why This PhD Stands Out

This is not a conventional PhD. It is designed for researchers who want to:

  • Explore how AI can be co-created with young people
  • Develop innovative qualitative and participatory research methods
  • Influence ethical AI design and policy recommendations
  • Work within a multidisciplinary, international research environment

By focusing on co-creation, this project ensures that AI systems better reflect the real, dynamic experiences of youth rather than relying solely on static datasets.

Research Focus

The IAmAI project tackles a critical issue in AI development—the mismatch between generalized data models and individual lived experiences.

Your research will:

  • Investigate interactions between youth and AI systems
  • Develop and test participatory research methodologies
  • Reimagine personalization in AI tools such as learning platforms and generative AI chatbots
  • Translate findings into actionable policy and methodological recommendations

Key Responsibilities

During the PhD, you will:

  • Conduct high-quality qualitative research
  • Publish in internationally recognized academic journals
  • Present findings at global conferences
  • Support teaching in psychology and educational sciences programs

What You Will Gain

This position offers a highly supportive academic environment with opportunities to grow professionally and personally:

  • Full funding for four years
  • Access to international conferences, workshops, and training
  • Collaboration with leading interdisciplinary researchers
  • Additional benefits such as transport reimbursement, insurance, and eco-vouchers
  • A dynamic, multicultural research setting in Leuven, Belgium

Ideal Candidate Profile

Applicants should have:

  • A Master’s degree in educational sciences, psychology, or a related field
  • Strong interest in qualitative research methods
  • Passion for youth engagement and participatory approaches
  • Interest in AI and ethical research practices
  • Excellent communication skills
  • Fluency in Dutch and English

Experience in academic writing and familiarity with qualitative data collection and analysis are strong advantages.

Location and Start Date

  • Location: Leuven, Belgium
  • Start Date: October 2026 (flexible upon agreement)

Application Process

Applicants are required to submit:

  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Motivation letter
  • Academic transcripts
  • Optional writing sample (thesis or research paper)

Applications must be submitted via the official KU Leuven online platform.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for this PhD position is May 31, 2026 (23:59 CET).

Final Thoughts

This PhD offers a rare opportunity to contribute to the ethical and inclusive future of AI by directly involving young people in its development. If you are passionate about research, education, and technology—and want to make a meaningful impact—this program provides the perfect platform.

The post Fully Funded PhD on Youth & AI at KU Leuven (IAmAI Project) – Apply by May 31, 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Zeitz MOCAA & UWC Museum Fellowship Programme 2027: Fully Funded Opportunity for Emerging African Art Professionals
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The Zeitz Museum of Contemporary Art Africa (Zeitz MOCAA), in partnership with the University of the Western Cape (UWC), has officially opened applications for its prestigious Museum Fellowship Programme 2027. This highly competitive, fully immersive fellowship is designed to empower the next generation of African art and museum professionals through hands-on training, academic development, and […]

The post Zeitz MOCAA & UWC Museum Fellowship Programme 2027: Fully Funded Opportunity for Emerging African Art Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Zeitz Museum of Contemporary Art Africa (Zeitz MOCAA), in partnership with the University of the Western Cape (UWC), has officially opened applications for its prestigious Museum Fellowship Programme 2027. This highly competitive, fully immersive fellowship is designed to empower the next generation of African art and museum professionals through hands-on training, academic development, and real-world experience.

If you are passionate about contemporary African art, curatorial practice, and museum studies, this fellowship offers a transformative opportunity to shape the future of the continent’s cultural and heritage landscape.

About the Fellowship Programme

The Zeitz MOCAA & UWC Museum Fellowship Programme is a 12-month, full-time programme running from February 2027 to January 2028 in Cape Town, South Africa. It combines practical museum experience with academic coursework, culminating in a BA Honours degree from UWC upon successful completion.

Fellows will gain direct exposure to various museum departments, including:

  • Curatorial Practice
  • Exhibition Design
  • Registrar & Collection Management
  • Art Education
  • Institutional Advancement

Participants will work closely with industry experts, contributing to exhibitions, research, public programming, and publishing initiatives.

What the Fellowship Offers

This fellowship is designed to provide both academic and professional growth. Selected fellows will receive:

  • Full tuition coverage
  • Accommodation
  • Monthly stipend
  • Basic health insurance
  • Hands-on experience in a leading contemporary art museum
  • Academic mentorship and supervision

Please note that travel and visa costs are not covered.

Eligibility Criteria

To be considered for the programme, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Hold a Bachelor’s degree in art, heritage, museum studies, or a related humanities field (or equivalent experience)
  • Demonstrate knowledge of contemporary African art and heritage
  • Have 2–3 years of relevant work experience
  • Possess strong writing and public speaking skills
  • Show clear interest in an art-related career
  • Be proficient in English
  • Be a citizen of an African country

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit the following documents:

  • One-page motivation letter
  • Full CV
  • 250-word biography
  • A minimum 1000-word original writing sample
  • Letter of recommendation
  • High-resolution portrait photo
  • Academic certificates and transcripts

All documents must be submitted in English and follow the required naming format.

Important Application Guidelines

  • Only the first 100 applications will be considered
  • Applications must be submitted online
  • Incomplete or late submissions will not be accepted
  • Applicants must carefully follow all instructions and word limits

Deadline

The application deadline is 30 June 2026.

Successful applicants will be contacted by early August 2026.

Why You Should Apply

This fellowship is more than just a training programme—it is a gateway into Africa’s evolving art ecosystem. Fellows will gain invaluable experience, expand their professional networks, and develop the skills needed to lead in curatorial practice, research, and museum management.

Whether you aim to work in galleries, museums, cultural institutions, or academia, this programme provides a strong foundation for a successful career in the arts.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should complete the online application form and upload all required documents before the deadline. Early application is strongly encouraged due to the limited number of slots considered.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. 

The post Zeitz MOCAA & UWC Museum Fellowship Programme 2027: Fully Funded Opportunity for Emerging African Art Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Internet Society Foundation Research Grant Program 2026: Up to $500,000 in Funding for Global Internet Research
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The Internet Society Foundation has officially opened applications for its highly anticipated Research Grant Program 2026, offering funding between $200,000 and $500,000 to support innovative research projects that explore the evolving role of the Internet in society. This prestigious global program is designed to empower researchers and institutions to generate practical, open-access knowledge that can […]

The post Internet Society Foundation Research Grant Program 2026: Up to $500,000 in Funding for Global Internet Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Internet Society Foundation has officially opened applications for its highly anticipated Research Grant Program 2026, offering funding between $200,000 and $500,000 to support innovative research projects that explore the evolving role of the Internet in society.

This prestigious global program is designed to empower researchers and institutions to generate practical, open-access knowledge that can shape policies, improve digital ecosystems, and create a more inclusive, secure, and sustainable Internet for everyone.

💡 About the Research Grant Program

The Research Grant Program supports forward-thinking research aligned with the Internet Society Foundation’s 2030 strategic vision—a world where everyone has access to affordable, reliable, and secure Internet connectivity.

Over the past five years, the program has:

  • Funded 70+ research projects
  • Invested over $22 million globally
  • Influenced policy decisions, industry practices, and technological innovation

The program places strong emphasis on collaboration, diversity, and real-world impact, particularly encouraging researchers from the Global South and underrepresented communities.

🎯 Key Focus Areas

Applicants must align their proposals with at least one of the following themes:

1. Inclusive Internet

Research addressing barriers such as affordability, gender inequality, geography, and digital literacy that limit Internet access and participation.

2. Greening the Internet

Projects exploring the environmental impact of Internet infrastructure, energy consumption, and climate resilience.

3. Measuring Meaningful Connectivity

Studies focused on redefining connectivity beyond access—examining how Internet use enables real participation in economic, social, and civic life.

4. A Trustworthy Internet

Research on Internet security, reliability, accountability, and emerging risks that affect trust in digital systems.

💰 Funding Details

  • Up to $200,000 for independent researchers
  • Up to $500,000 for organizations and institutions

Funding supports applied, open research that addresses real-world challenges and is accessible to the global scientific community.

✅ Eligibility Criteria

  • Independent researchers must hold a postgraduate degree (Master’s or PhD) and demonstrate prior research output
  • Public research institutions must be non-profit (or equivalent) and aligned with the Foundation’s mission
  • Projects must comply with ethical standards and local regulations
  • Strong encouragement for applicants from underrepresented and Global Majority communities

🧠 Selection Criteria

Applications will be evaluated based on:

  • Alignment with thematic focus areas
  • Innovation and originality
  • Practical impact and policy relevance
  • Collaboration and interdisciplinary approach
  • Intersectional research design

📅 Important Deadline

  • Application Opens: April 7, 2026
  • Application Deadline: May 22, 2026 (21:00 UTC)

Late or incomplete applications will not be considered.

📝 How to Apply

Applicants must submit their proposals through the Foundation’s online grant management system (Fluxx). Key steps include:

  1. Register on the platform at least 24 hours before the deadline
  2. Prepare and submit a complete application in English, French, or Spanish
  3. Ensure compliance with all eligibility and documentation requirements

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more grantopportunities, click here. 

📢 Why You Should Apply

This program is more than just funding—it’s an opportunity to:

  • Influence global Internet policy and governance
  • Contribute to cutting-edge research and innovation
  • Collaborate with a diverse, international research community
  • Create real-world impact in digital inclusion and sustainability

🔗 Useful Links

  • Apply via the official platform (Fluxx)
  • Attend info sessions (April 20 & 21, 2026)
  • Contact: foundation@isocfoundation.org

📌 Final Thoughts

If you are passionate about shaping the future of the Internet and addressing global digital challenges, the Internet Society Foundation Research Grant Program 2026 offers a powerful platform to turn your ideas into impactful solutions.

The post Internet Society Foundation Research Grant Program 2026: Up to $500,000 in Funding for Global Internet Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
